title: Concrete Garden

inputs.title: Concrete Garden
inputs.theme: growing something beautiful out of a place that was supposed to break you

brief: Make a hook first boom-bap hip-hop song titled "Concrete Garden". Center it on: growing, something, beautiful, out, place, was, supposed, break. Keep it grounded and specific — not corny. Tempo 88-96 BPM. References (feel, never copied): none given.
conceptSummary: Boom-Bap Hip-Hop record for the block: growing something beautiful out of a place that was supposed to break you — delivered defiant, hopeful, hard-earned, voiced as gritty.

hookOptions.text: Still standing where the harbor used to be
Every step a promise that I hustle
I been heavy but I never let it show
For block, I push through the ember
Tell block I made it out the table

chosenHook.text: Tell block I made it out the table

lines: Block, this one's for you

lines: Tell block I made it out the table

sections.label: Verse 2

Stakes got higher, place became the flow
Put the thorn down, reaching for the flow
No turning back, I want the ash and the glow

lines: Made my peace with glow and the frame
Every promise of mine became a name

finalLyrics:
[Intro]
Block, this one's for you

[Hook]
Tell block I made it out the table
Tell block I made it out the table
And block know I pray for it
Tell block I made it out the table

[Verse 1]
Came up on place, dreaming of the trade
Grew up on photograph, chasing the grade
It started with the compass and the art
Nothing in my hands but shadow and the part

[Hook]
Tell block I made it out the table
Tell block I made it out the table
And block know I pray for it
Tell block I made it out the table

[Verse 2]
Took that photograph and turned it to a glow
Stakes got higher, place became the flow
Put the thorn down, reaching for the flow
No turning back, I want the ash and the glow

[Bridge]
Made my peace with glow and the frame
Every promise of mine became a name

[Hook]
Tell block I made it out the table
Tell block I made it out the table
And block know I pray for it
Tell block I made it out the table

arrangement: Intro: sparse, just the lead motif + vinyl
Hook: full drums in, 808 glide, double the vocal
Verse: pull the pad, let the knock breathe
Bridge: drop drums, swell, then re-enter on the one
Outro: filter the beat down, leave the hook ringing
production.genreBlend: boom-bap hip-hop core with melodic/emotional top line
production.mixVibe: wide, warm low end, vocal up front, slight tape saturation

vocals.delivery: defiant, hopeful, hard-earned delivery, conversational on verses, lifted on the hook
vocals.doublesAndStacks: Double the hook, stack harmonies on the last line of each chorus.

visuals.albumCoverPrompt: Album cover, square 1:1. Cinematic, defiant, hopeful, hard-earned mood. Subject reflects: Boom-Bap Hip-Hop record for the block: growing something beautiful out of a place that was supposed to break you — delivered defiant, hopeful, hard-earned, voiced as gritty.. Visual motifs: growing, something, beautiful, out. Color: deep shadows, one warm accent light. Film grain, 35mm. No text, no logos, no real-artist likeness. Original styling.
visuals.musicVideoPrompt: Cinematic 16:9 music video. Concept: Boom-Bap Hip-Hop record for the block: growing something beautiful out of a place that was supposed to break you — delivered defiant, hopeful, hard-earned, voiced as gritty.. defiant, hopeful, hard-earned tone, boom-bap hip-hop energy. Grounded, not corny; real locations, natural light with one stylized accent. Original — no copyrighted footage or artist mimicry.
sceneIdeas: Opening: slow 16:9 push-in on the subject, defiant, hopeful, hard-earned light, city at dawn.
Hook: handheld, performance energy, neon reflections.
Bridge: single locked-off wide, subject still while the world moves.
Outro: pull back to reveal the place it all started.
shortFormClipIdeas: Behind-the-scenes studio reaction to the drop
Single-take walk synced to the bridge

agentOutputs.name: HERMES Conductor
agentOutputs.finding: Brief locked: hook-first boom-bap hip-hop for the block.

data.brief: Make a hook first boom-bap hip-hop song titled "Concrete Garden". Center it on: growing, something, beautiful, out, place, was, supposed, break. Keep it grounded and specific — not corny. Tempo 88-96 BPM. References (feel, never copied): none given.
data.concept: Boom-Bap Hip-Hop record for the block: growing something beautiful out of a place that was supposed to break you — delivered defiant, hopeful, hard-earned, voiced as gritty.

angles.angle: Tell "growing" through a letter you'll never send
angles.why: An oblique frame forces fresh, specific images instead of the obvious ones.

angles.angle: Anchor the whole song to one image: "night"
angles.why: A single concrete anchor from an unrelated domain (time) gives the song a spine and a surprise.

angles.angle: Flip the perspective — write it as an object that outlived the person
angles.why: Re-narrating from an unexpected vantage is the DMN move that lifts originality.

agentOutputs.name: Emotion Scanner
agentOutputs.finding: defiance · clarity 100/100 → turn toward the tenderness underneath it.

emotion.guidance: Lead with defiance (bright, high-intensity), then turn toward the tenderness underneath it for depth. Render the feeling in a concrete image, don't name it.
